release.publicApiMeaning: PUBLIC_API.md files guard against accidental exports; they are not API or schema freezes.

invariants: Already-clear algebra must become type-proof or boot-proof.
Every ledger fact has one commit primitive as its source generator.
Every algebra has one code source.
Runtime validation is only for unknown external input, not internally constructed states.
Every fact has exactly one writer.
Do not derive intent from actual.

holds.text: MCP registry support is not part of the {releaseLine} surface; it remains a hold until discovery, install, authority, and material contracts converge across products.

packages.name: @agent-os/kernel
packages.role: pure claim, boundary, material, projection, AgentSchema, JSON Schema dialect, tool, context, and type algebra
packages.boundary: platform-free claim/material/boundary/projection/AgentSchema/tool algebra plus raw JSON Schema dialect for non-LLM boundaries
packages.readmeStatus: {releaseLine} active development. Public exports are listed for package ownership; they are not frozen.
packages.apiStatus: {releaseLine} active development. Public exports are listed for accidental export control only; this is not an API freeze.

packages.name: @agent-os/runtime
packages.role: Effect Tag runtime programs, runtime services, projections, workspace-job observability joins, and backend-neutral durable trigger authoring algebra
packages.boundary: backend-neutral runtime Tag contracts, service implementations, projections, cross-carrier observability composition, trigger registry contracts, and trigger authoring types
packages.readmeStatus: {releaseLine} active development. Public exports are listed for package ownership; they are not frozen.
packages.apiStatus: {releaseLine} active development. Public exports are listed for accidental export control only; this is not an API freeze.

packages.name: @agent-os/runtime-protocol
packages.role: runtime-owned serializable submit, admission, authored manifest, manifest projection, ledger identity, and runtime event vocabulary
packages.boundary: runtime protocol vocabulary and pure projections only; runtime Effect services, generated clients, and control flow live outside @agent-os/runtime-protocol
packages.readmeStatus: Runtime protocol package. It owns serializable runtime vocabulary consumed across runtime, backend, composer, and ops boundaries.
packages.apiStatus: Runtime protocol package. Public exports are limited to runtime DTOs, authored manifest DTOs, pure manifest/admission projections, ledger identity types, and runtime ledger event vocabulary.

packages.name: @agent-os/telemetry-protocol
packages.role: trace context and backend-neutral telemetry event tree vocabulary
packages.boundary: telemetry semantic vocabulary and validation only; OTLP and vendor sinks are wire adapters
packages.readmeStatus: Telemetry protocol package. It owns trace context and agentOS telemetry event tree vocabulary.
packages.apiStatus: Telemetry protocol package. Public exports are limited to trace context validation and backend-neutral telemetry event tree vocabulary.

packages.name: @agent-os/telemetry-otlp
packages.role: OTLP projection adapter for agentOS telemetry and ledger facts
packages.boundary: OTLP and OpenTelemetry semantic-convention mapping only; telemetry source vocabulary lives in telemetry-protocol
packages.readmeStatus: Telemetry wire adapter package. It maps agentOS telemetry semantics to OTLP span payloads.
packages.apiStatus: Telemetry OTLP adapter public exports are limited to the mapping version, OTLP span DTOs, and pure projection function.

packages.name: @agent-os/llm-protocol
packages.role: provider-neutral LLM request, response, route, and wire descriptor vocabulary
packages.boundary: LLM protocol vocabulary and provider-neutral wire descriptors; concrete provider protocols are provider implementations
packages.readmeStatus: LLM protocol package. It owns provider-neutral LLM request, response, route, and wire descriptor vocabulary.
packages.apiStatus: LLM protocol package. Public exports are limited to provider-neutral LLM protocol vocabulary and wire descriptor helpers.

packages.name: @agent-os/backend-protocol
packages.role: storage-free backend port DTOs, protocol constants, intent-pointer payload parsers, retry policy, dispatch protocol, resource/quota projection shapes, and handler fanout policy
packages.boundary: backend protocol algebra shared by runtime services and concrete backend implementations
packages.readmeStatus: Backend protocol package. It is backend-only shared algebra, not a general-purpose core package.
packages.apiStatus: Backend protocol package. Public exports are limited to storage-free backend protocol semantics shared by runtime services and backend implementations.

packages.name: @agent-os/backend-cloudflare-do
packages.role: Cloudflare Durable Object backend, mount interpreter, and workspace-job host composition for agentOS runtime
packages.boundary: Cloudflare DO storage, alarm, SSE, dispatch, workspace-job response/profile composition, backend mount interpretation, and binding materialization
packages.readmeStatus: Backend package. It is the only substrate package that imports `cloudflare:workers`.
packages.apiStatus: Backend package. Public exports include the Durable Object factory, mount interpreter, backend runtime clients, workspace host helpers, dispatch targets, and backend-specific configuration types; app facade constructors and SubmitSpec hand-wiring are internal test substrate.

packages.name: @agent-os/backend-node-postgres

packages.boundary: Node process plus Postgres storage, due-work claiming, dispatch retry, and backend-neutral ledger persistence
packages.readmeStatus: Backend package. It is the Node/Postgres production interpreter used as the second backend-neutral proof backend.
packages.apiStatus: Backend package. Public exports are limited to the Node/Postgres backend interpreter and backend-specific configuration types.

packages.apiStatus: Backend package for runtime contract tests. Public exports are limited to in-memory backend state, runtime Layer constructors, and Tag Live implementations.
